campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies in the city have to pay. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax amounts, and the council then debated the issue in public before voting on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Finance department presentation on new tax structure
Public council deliberation and voting
Municipal code amendments for fiscal year changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about an issue before the council. After hearing public input, the council introduced a new rule (ordinance) and decided to skip the usual first reading process to move things along faster. The council then scheduled this rule to be voted on and finalized at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input and feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Specific topic of ordinance not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting notes from earlier in April, review the town's finances for February, and authorize the Town Manager to sign several construction and professional service contracts. The council also moved forward with approving improvements to town infrastructure.
keyTopics: Approved financial report showing February 2026 budget and investments
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved infrastructure improvement projects for the town
Authorized multi-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several routine matters, including official meeting records and a plan to replace an aging elevator at a city terminal. The council also reviewed proposals for cleaning up homeless encampments, updated an advertising agreement with Clear Channel for digital billboards on city property, and approved city funding for a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade underway
Encampment Cleanup Services - city requesting proposals from contractors
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel - updates to billboard contracts on city property
Light the Night for Fallen Firefighters - memorial event funding approved for District 7

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes, hear public comments from residents, and review grant funding requests from neighborhood groups for the 2026-27 budget year. The council discussed which neighborhood projects should receive city funding and reviewed attendance at neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Neighborhood meeting attendance tracking
Budget allocation for community projects

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to watch and comment either in person at City Hall or online.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Study session format for council review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss a potential property deal involving a building at 10480 Finch Avenue. City leaders Tina Kapoor and Floy Andrews are negotiating with the Cupertino Union School District over the price and payment terms for this property. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and discussed plans for getting grant funding.
keyTopics: Property negotiation at 10480 Finch Avenue between City and School District
Discussion of purchase price and payment terms
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large decorative signs to be put on buildings around Levi's Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also approved a new contract with the city's firefighters that covers 2026 through 2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighter contract agreement approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council held a meeting to plan for the city's spending over the next several years. They discussed the budget for 2026-27, approved a plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District area, and updated the rules for how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
Next year's city budget presentation (2026-27)
New parks and trails coming to Innovation District
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and rules

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to consider a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine single-family homes and seven smaller accessory units, served by a new private street. The council also handled routine business including electing new leadership, reviewing schedules, and staff reports.
keyTopics: Plan to build 9 new houses + 7 small rental units on Old Trace Road
Subdivision of 1-acre vacant lot into residential development
Election of council chair and vice chair
Staff director report and meeting schedule updates
